title: hibiscus manihot var. zenkeri
intro: Hibiscus manihot var. zenkeri, also known as the elephant ear tree or white hibiscus, is a tropical plant species belonging to the mallow family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hibiscus manihot var. zenkeri include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically for skin and wound care.
